name: Protobuf.Utils.Bytes
comment:
Utility methods for Bytes needed in the Protobuf/gRPC context.

@docs jsonEncoder, jsonDecoder

values.name: jsonDecoder
values.comment: Decode bytes from the format expected by JSON-based gRPC APIs (base64 standard with padding)

values.name: jsonEncoder
values.comment: Encode bytes to a the format expected by JSON-based gRPC APIs (base64 standard with padding)

name: Protobuf.Utils.Dict
comment:
Utility methods for Dict needed in the Protobuf/gRPC context.

@docs dictDecoder

values.name: dictDecoder
values.comment:
Decode a dict from JSON with non-String key type.
This is done by first decoding into a dict/list of key/value pairs with Strings as keys
and then decoding each key while inserting them into a new dict.
values.type: (String.String -> Result.Result String.String comparable) -> Json.Decode.Decoder v -> Json.Decode.Decoder (Dict.Dict comparable v)

name: Protobuf.Utils.Float
comment:
Utility methods for Float needed in the Protobuf/gRPC context.

@docs stringOrFloatJsonDecoder

values.name: stringOrFloatJsonDecoder
values.comment:
Decode a float from a JSON float (1.23, 2.3e4)
or a String formatted in the same way ("1.23", "2.3e4").
Also accepts the special strings "Infinity", "-Infinity" and "NaN" resulting in
`1/0`, `-1/0` and `0/0` respectively.

name: Protobuf.Utils.Int32
comment:
Utility methods for Int64 needed in the Protobuf/gRPC context.

@docs int32JsonDecoder, uint32JsonDecoder

values.name: int32JsonDecoder
values.comment:
Decodes an Int from a signed integer JSON string (i.e. {"key": "-123456789"})
or a JSON number literal (i.e. {"key": -123}).
Fails if the number is outside of the int32 range (-2^31 to 2^31 - 1)

values.name: uint32JsonDecoder
values.comment:
Decodes an Int from an unsigned integer JSON string (i.e. {"key": "123456789"})
or a JSON number literal (i.e. {"key": 123}).
Fails if the number is outside of the int32 range (0 to 2^32 - 1)

name: Protobuf.Utils.Int64

values.name: fromInt
values.comment:
Constructs an Int64 from an Int. The Int can be bigger than 32-bit,
you can expect Integers in the safe int range in JS (-(2^53 - 1) to 2^53 - 1) to work.

values.name: fromSignedString
values.comment:
Parse a signed Int64 from its string representation

    fromSignedString "10"
        --> Just (Int64 { higher = 0, lower = 10 })

    fromSignedString "-10"
        --> Just (Int64 { higher = -1, lower = -10 })

    fromSignedString "not-a-number"
        --> Nothing

    fromSignedString "9223372036854775807"
        --> Just (Int64 { higher = 2147483647, lower = -1 })

    fromSignedString "-9223372036854775808"
        --> Just (Int64 { higher = -2147483648, lower = 0 })

    fromSignedString "-9223372036854775809"
        --> Nothing

values.name: fromUnsignedString
values.comment:
Parse an unsigned Int64 from its string representation

    fromUnsignedString "10"
        --> Just (Int64 { higher = 0, lower = 10 })

    fromUnsignedString "-10"
        --> Nothing

    fromUnsignedString "not-a-number"
        --> Nothing

    fromUnsignedString "9223372036854775807"
        --> Just (Int64 { higher = 2147483647, lower = -1 })

    fromUnsignedString "18446744073709551615"
        --> Just (Int64 { higher = -1, lower = -1 })

    fromUnsignedString "18446744073709551616"
        --> Nothing

values.name: int64JsonDecoder
values.comment:
Decodes an Int64 from a signed integer JSON string (i.e. {"key": "-123456789"})
or a JSON number literal (i.e. {"key": -123})

values.name: int64JsonEncoder
values.comment: Encodes an Int64 as a signed integer JSON string

values.name: toIntUnsafe
values.comment:
Attempts to convert the Int64 into a JS Int.
This should be safe for the safe int range in JS (-(2^53 - 1) to 2^53 - 1).
Expect weird results for anything outside that range.

values.name: toSignedString
values.comment:
Interpret a `Int64` as an unsigned integer, and give its string representation.

    toSignedString (fromInt 10)
        --> "10"

    toSignedString (fromInt -10)
        --> "-10"

values.name: toUnsignedString
values.comment:
Interpret a `Int64` as an unsigned integer, and give its string representation

    toUnsignedString (fromInt 10)
        --> "10"

    toUnsignedString (fromInt -10)
        --> "18446744073709551606"

values.name: uint64JsonDecoder
values.comment:
Decodes an Int64 from an unsigned integer JSON string (i.e. {"key": "123456789"})
or a JSON number literal (i.e. {"key": 123})

values.name: uint64JsonEncoder
values.comment: Encodes an Int64 as an unsigned integer JSON string

name: Protobuf.Utils.Timestamp
comment:
Conversions between the Protobuf Well-Known Type "Timestamp" and Elm's `Time.Posix`.

@docs posixToTimestamp, timestampToPosix, timestampJsonEncoder, timestampJsonDecoder

values.name: posixToTimestamp
values.comment: Convert a posix millisecond timestamp into the protobuf seconds/nanos representation

values.name: timestampJsonDecoder
values.comment: Custom JSON decoder for timestamps as ISO-8601 date strings

values.name: timestampJsonEncoder
values.comment: Custom JSON encoder for timestamps as ISO-8601 date strings

values.name: timestampToPosix
values.comment: Convert the protobuf seconds/nanos representation into a posix millisecond timestamp
